headline: STEM Enrollment Falls 8% While Arts Programs Grow in 2026
context: Public university STEM enrollment declined for the third consecutive year while humanities and social science programs expanded.

cards.big: STEM enrollment dropped from 48% to 40% of public university intake since 2023
cards.sub: Government target of 60% STEM students by 2030 is now virtually unreachable

cards.big: Only 35% of SPM students achieved grade C or above in Additional Mathematics 2025
cards.sub: Weak secondary school preparation deters students from STEM pathways

cards.big: Starting salaries for STEM graduates average RM2,900 versus RM2,700 for arts in 2025
cards.sub: Minimal wage premium provides little financial incentive for harder programs

cards.big: India's STEM push increased enrollment 35% through targeted scholarships since 2020
cards.sub: Financial incentives combined with career guarantees drove participation

cards.big: Students are rational—they avoid STEM when the reward does not match the difficulty
Should STEM graduates receive loan forgiveness as incentive for pursuing difficult programs
